compiled_state.title: Implement bash-native write_commit_event (flock, printf-JSON, direct git commit)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Task

Implement bash-native write_commit_event in ticket-lib-api.sh replacing the 3 Python subprocess calls.

### Architecture decision

Choose at implementation time (prefer shim pattern):
- Option A (in-place): Replace write_commit_event in ticket-lib.sh directly
- Option B (shim): Keep ticket-lib.sh as a thin shim that sources ticket-lib-api.sh and re-exports write_commit_event

### Bash-native write_commit_event

1. Locking: flock -x -w 30 lockfile with LOCK_EX semantics and 2-retry loop on timeout
2. JSON generation: escape-safe printf-based generation byte-for-exact compatible with python json.dumps(ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':'), sort_keys=True):
   - Sort keys alphabetically
   - No trailing comma
   - Unicode pass-through (no uXXXX encoding for non-ASCII)
   - Special chars escaped: backslash, quote, newline, tab, etc.
3. Direct git commit: git -C tracker_dir commit -m message (skip-hooks flag handled separately)
4. Preserve exact function signature, exit codes (0=success, 1=lock-timeout), and all side effects

### Testing Mode

GREEN — make test-ticket-write-commit-event.sh pass (tests in e253-eecd).

## File Impact

- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh (UPDATE — add bash-native write_commit_event)
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh (UPDATE — either in-place replacement or add source shim)

## Acceptance

- test-ticket-write-commit-event.sh exits 0 (GREEN)
- All 9 existing scripts that source ticket-lib.sh pass their existing tests
- test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh passes unmodified
